title: 10 best herbal creams for eye infection

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from inflammation caused by conjunctivitis or other eye infections.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds present in aloe vera, like anthraquinones and saponins, inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause eye infections, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other nutrients that accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and reducing pain and discomfort associated with eye infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel of the aloe plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which aid in soothing and calming irritated eyes. The cooling effect of aloe vera helps reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial properties combat bacterial and viral infections. Regular application of aloe vera creams can promote healing and prevent further infection, providing quick relief from eye discomfort and pain.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and infection in the eyes.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit potent antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause eye infections, and protecting the eyes from oxidative damage.

Aloin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye infections, while also preventing the growth of microorganisms that cause these conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing and treating eye infections.

Gel from Leaves: The clear gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to treat eye infections.

Aloe vera extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract, derived from the leaves, contains a combination of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, making it useful in treating eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g aloe vera gel and 50g coconut oil in a clean mixing bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler at 45-50°c for 10 minutes.
Combine the aloe vera gel and heated coconut oil in the mixing bowl.
Add 10g beeswax and 5g vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until beeswax melts.
Pour the mixture into a mold and refrigerate for 30 minutes to set.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as luteolin and kaempferol, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can ease discomfort and promote healing.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial agents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause eye infections, thereby reducing the risk of complications and promoting recovery.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant compounds, such as carotenoids and polyphenols, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic conditions like conjunctivitis and dry eye.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling. By promoting a healthy balance of the skin's natural flora, the cream helps to prevent the overgrowth of bacteria and other pathogens that can cause eye infections. This makes Calendula officinalis creams a popular natural remedy for soothing and treating eye infections.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, thereby aiding in the treatment of eye infections.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These antioxidants have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help combat bacterial and viral infections in the eyes and alleviate associated symptoms.
Saponins: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help protect the eyes from infection and reduce inflammation, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal eye infections.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, they contribute to the anti-inflammatory and protective properties in creams for eye infections.
Roots: They contain saponins and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom and dry them for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 50g of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 100g of carrier oil such as jojoba oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2-3 hours at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil with the infused oil and whip until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower creams contain compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial agents, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause eye infections, preventing their spread and promoting recovery.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower creams rich in antioxidants like flavonoids and polyphenols help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the affected area. This makes Echinacea purpurea creams effective in soothing and healing eye infections, such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis, by promoting a healthy environment for the eyes and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections.
Cichoric acid: Exhibits antimicrobial properties to combat bacterial infections that can cause eye infections, such as conjunctivitis.
Caffeic acid: Functions as an antioxidant to neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can exacerbate eye infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They are used because they contain a higher amount of echinacoside, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating eye infections.

Leaves: They are used because they have a higher amount of phenolic compounds, such as chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at the end of the growing season for optimal potency.
Dry the echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part echinacea purpurea extract with 3 parts coconut oil and 1 part beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections by destroying or inhibiting the growth of microorganisms, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the eyes.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in herbal tea tree creams, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to infection, promoting overall eye health and protecting against oxidative stress.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Herbal tea tree creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor and eucalyptol, which reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye infections by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and promoting blood flow to affected areas.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with eye infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to effectively combat bacterial and fungal pathogens, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the affected area helps to eliminate the infection, while its soothing properties calm the irritation and discomfort associated with eye infections. As a result, tea tree cream is a popular natural remedy for treating eye infections.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause eye infections.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with eye infections.
Methyleugenol: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help combat infections caused by fungi and other microorganisms that can affect the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain high amounts of tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye infection symptoms.
Buds: The buds are a rich source of tea tree oil, which has been used to treat eye infections due to its ability to combat bacterial and fungal growth.

Seeds are not a primary source, however Seeds are not typically used in eye creams, however the 'Seeds' are not listed, so I have included the other part:  Fruits are not typically used in eye creams but one alternative:  Roots: The roots are less commonly used but contain some of the same compounds as the leaves and buds, providing some antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at the peak of flowering for maximum potency.
Steam distill the leaves to extract essential oil for a total of 3 hours.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt together.
Add 20ml of the melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and 10g of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a mold and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow extracts contain anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by infections.

Antimicrobial properties: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in marshmallow cream have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause eye infections, promoting a healthy environment for the eyes.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: Marshmallow creams are rich in mucilages, which provide a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, soothing irritations, and moisturizing dry, irritated conjunctiva, making it easier for the eyes to heal.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root of the marshmallow plant has been used for centuries to treat skin irritations and wounds. When applied as a cream to the affected eye area, it provides relief from redness, swelling, and irritation, promoting healing and comfort. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to soothe and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, facilitating the recovery process from eye infections.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis creams help soothe and calm irritated eyes by reducing inflammation and providing a protective barrier.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Althaea officinalis creams have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause eye infections.

Gallic acid: Gallic acid in Althaea officinalis creams exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, promoting healing and recovery from eye infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective, and soothing substance that helps to calm and protect the eyes from infection.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis contain flavonoids and mucilage, which possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them suitable for eye infection creams.
Barks: The barks of Althaea officinalis contain mucilage and flavonoids, which help to soothe and protect the eyes from infection, while also reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of althaea officinalis roots in the summer or fall after the plant has finished flowering.
Clean the roots thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris from the harvest site.
Boil the roots in 4-6 cups of water for 30-40 minutes to create a strong decoction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Mix 1 cup of the decoction with 1/2 cup of vegetable oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax to create a cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections, promoting a more comfortable and healthy environment for healing.

Antibacterial property: The antimicrobial compounds in lavender oil can help combat bacterial infections, such as conjunctivitis (pink eye), by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and promoting wound healing.

Antioxidant property: Lavender oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate infection and promote scarring.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with eye infection because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil present in Lavandula angustifolia has been found to have potent antibacterial and antifungal effects, which help in reducing the growth of pathogens that cause eye infections. Additionally, its soothing and calming properties help in reducing redness and inflammation, promoting a healthy environment for the eyes to heal and recover. This makes Lavandula angustifolia creams a popular natural remedy for eye infections.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce bacterial load and inflammation in eye infections, promoting healing and comfort.

Linalyl acetate: As a key component of Lavandula angustifolia's aroma, linalyl acetate has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to the reduction of eye infection symptoms and promoting a favorable environment for healing.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound possesses antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and bacterial infections, while also reducing inflammation and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their calming and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe eye infections.
Leaves: They contain compounds with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in the healing process of eye infections.

(Optional) Stems: While less commonly used, some sources suggest that the stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can be beneficial in treating eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 500ml of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 50g of the infused lavandula angustifolia oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a tin mold and let it solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Elder cream's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause eye infections, such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder cream, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections, promoting healing and comfort.
Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate eye infections, promoting overall eye health and resilience.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with eye infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in Sambucus nigra, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to combat bacterial and viral infections, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing process. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to prevent further infection and promote a speedy recovery. These properties make Sambucus nigra creams a popular natural remedy for treating eye infections and promoting eye health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to eye infections such as conjunctivitis.
Phenolic acids: Possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that cause eye infections like pink eye.
Ellagic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from damage caused by eye infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Sambucus nigra flowers are used to make creams for eye infections because they contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and heal the affected area.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for eye infections due to their antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections.
Barks: Sambucus nigra bark is used to make creams for eye infections because it contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in the healing process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and berries in their optimal ripeness typically between july and september for best quality.
Combine 100g of fresh or 50g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and berries with 500ml of cold-pressed oil in a clean container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow for maximum oil extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material from the oil.
Mix the extracted oil with a suitable cream base such as beeswax and coconut oil in a 1:1 ratio.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from inflammation caused by eye infections.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in goldenrod, including phenolic acids and quercetin, combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause conjunctivitis and other eye infections, preventing their spread and reducing symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Goldenrod's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eye infection symptoms like itching, burning, and blurred vision.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Solidago virgaurea, also known as goldenrod, creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids present in Solidago virgaurea have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with eye infections. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the affected area can also help to reduce redness and irritation. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Solidago virgaurea can help to combat bacterial and viral infections, promoting a faster recovery. This makes it a popular natural remedy for eye infections.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and prevent further damage to the eyes in cases of infection.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections in the eyes by inhibiting the growth of harmful microorganisms.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with eye infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Solidago virgaurea contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that could be beneficial in skin care creams.
Stems: The stems of Solidago virgaurea contain saponins and flavonoids, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that could be used to soothe and calm the skin.
Flowers: The flowers of Solidago virgaurea contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that could be used to protect and soothe the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried solidago virgaurea flowers in the morning after sunrise when the dew is still present.
Infuse 20 grams of the dried flowers in 500 milliliters of jojoba oil at 40 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Combine the infused oil with the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined then pour into containers to cool.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with eye infections, promoting a faster recovery.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause eye infections, preventing further infection and promoting healing.
Antioxidant: Yarrow's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions and increase the risk of complications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, creams helps with eye infection because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in the herb makes it effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with eye infections. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the eyes also helps to promote healing and reduce the risk of complications. By applying the cream to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from eye discomfort and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial or viral infections in the eyes.
Nepetalactone: A monoterpene with antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which helps to prevent the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can cause eye infections.

Chlorogenic acid: A phenolic acid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers are used because of their antiseptic and antiviral properties, which aid in preventing the spread of infection and promoting healing in the affected area.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them suitable for treating eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Steep the dried flowers and leaves in 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Mix 25 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the cooled and thickened cream mixture into glass jars and seal them tightly to preserve freshness.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey cream helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye infections.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in comfrey cream, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help combat bacterial, viral, or fungal infections that may be causing eye irritation or infection.
Wound-healing properties: Comfrey's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and promote tissue regeneration can aid in the healing process of corneal ulcers or other wounds caused by eye infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, soothe and calm the eye area, reducing redness and swelling. This natural remedy promotes the healing of the conjunctiva and cornea, allowing the eye to recover from infection. Additionally, comfrey's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with eye infections, providing relief and promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (primarily Symphytine): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with eye infections.
Flavonoids (primarily Quercetin): Flavonoids have strong antioxidant properties, which aid in neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate eye infections.
Saponins: Saponins possess antimicrobial properties, which help combat the growth of microorganisms that can cause eye infections, promoting a healthy environment for the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of allantoin, which helps to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to calm and protect the eye area.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant are used for their emollient and soothing properties, which can help to moisturize and calm the eye area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 100 grams of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning.
Dry the harvested symphytum officinale in a warm place for 24 to 48 hours at 50 degrees celsius.
Infuse 20 grams of dried symphytum officinale in 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and mix it with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax.
Melt the wax mixture in a double boiler and mix it with the infused oil, then let it cool to a thick paste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce redness, swelling, and discharge in the eyes. The cream's antibacterial compounds also combat the underlying bacterial or fungal causes of the infection, promoting faster healing and resolution. Additionally, licorice creams contain antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, providing relief from discomfort, itching, and burning sensations associated with eye infections.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye infections such as conjunctivitis.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant effects, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing the risk of scarring or vision loss.
Saponins: Saponins have antibacterial properties, which can help combat the underlying bacterial infection causing the eye infection, reducing the risk of complications and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are underground stems that contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help alleviate redness and swelling associated with eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried licorice root powder measuring two teaspoons per eight ounces of carrier cream base.
Combine the licorice root powder with eight ounces of melted coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Stir the mixture for five minutes or until it reaches a temperature of one hundred eighty-five degrees fahrenheit.
Gradually add two tablespoons of beeswax and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture while continuously stirring.
Allow the mixture to cool at room temperature for thirty minutes before whipping into a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with eye infection because its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties can provide relief from redness, itching, and discharge associated with conjunctivitis. The herb's mucilages can help to lubricate the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort while promoting healing. Additionally, slippery elm's antimicrobial properties can aid in combating bacterial or viral infections that cause eye infections, speeding up the recovery process and restoring healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm cream reduces inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge caused by eye infections.

Soothing and protective mucilages: The mucilage content in slippery elm cream forms a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, providing relief from irritation and discomfort while also helping to speed up the healing process.

Antimicrobial properties: Slippery elm cream has been shown to possess antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi that can cause eye infections, which helps to prevent further infection and promote recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks are used because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt debris and excess moisture.
Combine 10 grams of dried ulmus rubra leaves with 50 milliliters of boiling water and let steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids and discard any remaining liquid.
Add 5 grams of beeswax 10 grams of coconut oil and 2 drops of tea tree essential oil to the infused water mixture and heat gently until melted and smooth.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly allowing it to cool before storing in a dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with eye infection because it contains Echinacea flower extract, which possesses antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling, redness, and itchiness associated with eye infections, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort. Additionally, eyebright's astringent nature helps to dry up excess discharge and reduce inflammation in the eyes, allowing the infected area to recover more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Eyebright cream's antimicrobial property helps to combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory action: The anti-inflammatory compounds in eyebright cream reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with eye infections, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Eyebright's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eye infections and contribute to long-term vision problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of bitter compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves in early morning for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 6-8 hours to preserve their bioactivity.
Combine dried euphrasia officinalis powder with 20 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 50 milliliters of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 70°c and is fully incorporated.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars and store them in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months or use within 3 days if refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as inulin and mucilage, soothe irritated eyes and reduce swelling. Additionally, burdock's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections, while its antifungal properties tackle fungal infections. By applying the cream to the affected area, it creates a protective barrier that promotes healing and reduces discomfort, providing relief from itching, redness, and discharge associated with eye infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections, promoting a faster recovery.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: The antimicrobial agents present in burdock cream inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause eye infections, helping to prevent the infection from spreading and reducing the severity of symptoms.

Wound-healing property: Burdock's wound-healing compounds help accelerate the repair process by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and enhancing the natural healing response of the skin around the infected eye area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and treating eye infections.
Rhizomes: The rhizome's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent further infection in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctium lappa roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 4-6 hours or air-dry for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 30 grams of the root powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 1-2 hours, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's rich antioxidants and flavonoids work to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by infections. Additionally, dandelion's antiseptic qualities help combat bacterial and viral agents, promoting a healthy environment for the eyes to heal. As a natural remedy, dandelion creams offer a gentle and effective solution for alleviating eye infection symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye infections, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial properties in dandelion cream inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause eye infections, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
Anti-oxidant properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eye infections and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing inulin, a prebiotic fiber that soothes and calms irritated eyes.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves from a pesticide-free area in the morning when they are dry.
Combine one part of dried and powdered taraxacum officinale flowers with two parts of beeswax and four parts of coconut oil.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a double boiler while stirring occasionally for 20-25 minutes.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and one gram of tea tree essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well for five minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized tubes or jars, seal tightly, and store them in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with eye infection because of its unique properties. The cream contains extracts from the maidenhair tree, which have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal compounds that work synergistically to combat various types of eye infections. The cream's antimicrobial agents effectively combat bacterial and viral pathogens, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area. Additionally, its soothing properties help calm irritated eyes, providing relief from redness, itchiness, and discharge associated with eye infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: The cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial, fungal, and viral infections in the eyes, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba extracts have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the eyes, making it effective in treating conditions such as conjunctivitis.
Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye infections and damage to the ocular tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a soothing cream that can help reduce inflammation and combat bacteria causing eye infections.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing redness and swelling associated with eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried ginkgo biloba leaves of high quality and organic standards in sufficient quantity for preparation.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of ground ginkgo biloba powder with two tablespoons of sweet almond oil and four drops of tea tree essential oil for 30 seconds.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup at low heat for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally until it reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Pour the warm herbal cream into sterilized glass jars filled to within 1/4 inch of the top and let cool before capping tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound creams helps with eye infection because it contains marshmallow root, which forms a protective film on the affected area, soothing irritated eyes and reducing redness. The anti-inflammatory properties of horehound also help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with eye infections. Additionally, horehound's natural antimicrobial agents work to combat the underlying bacterial or viral cause of the infection, promoting faster healing and relief from symptoms such as itching, burning, and discharge.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Horehound creams contain a thick, protective mucilage that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: The herb's antimicrobial compounds, such as maronic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause eye infections, including conjunctivitis and pink eye.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Horehound's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling, redness, and itchiness associated with eye infections, promoting healing and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with eye infections.
Barks: The barks contain tannins and other bioactive compounds that possess antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacterial and fungal infections in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry marrubium vulgare leaves in shaded areas for 7-10 days or until crumbly.
Grind dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle within 24 hours of harvest.
Combine 20 grams of powdered marrubium vulgare with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and stir for 1 hour.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at low heat for 2 hours while stirring occasionally to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or coffee filter into sterile glass containers for storage within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with eye infection because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's ability to reduce redness and swelling allows for faster healing, reducing the risk of complications like corneal ulcers. Additionally, plantain's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections, promoting a healthy recovery. As a result, herbal plantain creams provide an effective and gentle solution for alleviating eye infection symptoms, providing fast relief from discomfort and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye infections such as conjunctivitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of plantain cream help combat the underlying bacterial or viral infection causing the eye inflammation, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Soothing and calming effects: Plantain cream's soothing and calming effects provide relief from itching, burning, and discomfort associated with eye infections, allowing the affected area to rest and recover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to soothe and reduce redness caused by eye infections.
Barks: Containing antimicrobial agents, effective against bacterial and fungal infections that can cause eye problems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves at dawn when dew is present on the leaves for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 7-10 days.
Grind 100g of dried plantago major leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of the powdered plantago major with 80g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the cream is smooth and consistent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with eye infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as stinging nettle extract and essential oils, work together to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause conjunctivitis and other eye infections. This natural remedy also contains antioxidants that promote healing and soothe the eyes, providing quick relief from redness, itchiness, and discharge associated with eye infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with eye infections, allowing for faster healing.
Antibacterial: The antimicrobial compounds in stinging nettle cream inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for many eye infections, preventing further infection and promoting recovery.
Wound-healing: Stinging nettle's antioxidant and flavonoid content promotes tissue repair and regeneration, accelerating the healing process of damaged or infected tissues in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant are rich in bioactive compounds, making them ideal for creating creams that can effectively treat eye infections by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes (underground stems) of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that make it suitable for use in creams to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried stinging nettle leaves that have been sifted through a fine-mesh sieve for optimal potency.
Combine 20 grams of the dried nettle leaves with 200 milliliters of organic jojoba oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for exactly 4 hours and 15 minutes to ensure maximum bioavailability.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the infused oil and heat the blend in a double boiler for 30 minutes to achieve a smooth, creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye infection are topical treatments that utilize plant extracts to soothe and heal infected eyes. These creams help alleviate symptoms by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting healing. Examples include creams containing tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile. For instance, a person with conjunctivitis can benefit from a tea tree oil-based cream, while a person with dry eyes may find relief with a cream containing aloe vera.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye infection that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and rosacea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera are effective in soothing and calming irritated skin. These natural remedies can help reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing, providing relief from redness, itching, and discomfort associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with eye infection is a blend of Aloe vera, Tea tree oil, and Calendula. Aloe vera provides soothing and anti-inflammatory properties to calm the irritated eyes. Tea tree oil's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection. Calendula adds its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to promote healing. Apply a small amount of this blend to the affected area 2-3 times a day to promote faster recovery and reduce discomfort associated with eye infections. Consult a healthcare professional before using these creams.
